reviews.review_msg: Unclear, grading is extremely tough & not suitable for a course that is an Introduction. Goes more in depth than 220, but this guy manages to make his explanations vague and depth-less. Don't waste time with the text, lecture notes or office hours where if you have a h/w question he'll just claim he can't give answers & regurgitates stuff from lect
He's a bad prof, avoid his class if you can. He doesn't even know what he's teaching.
I found James to be a pretty good prof - if you keep up on readings and go to office hours when you have a question, doing okay on homework and exams is totally achievable.

Took 222 with him in Summer 2013. He made me despise everything that syntax was. After taking 322 with a good professor, I'm starting to love syntax, but James is probably one of the worst professors I have ever had. He has difficulty explaining concepts, reads off a pdf, and makes the class feel very unwelcoming and uncomfortable. Avoid.

Really nice and funny person, not to mention he's handsome! As a prof he isn't the best- confuses students. Worst textbook ever.  Basically you'll be teaching yourself everything. Assignments (can do them as a group)  are worth 40% and you have to work for them, not easy! His exams are whatever,  just make sure to study well.

Nice guy. But he not a good prof. He explains thing in a that only he can understand and expects you to understand. Very lazy as well because he uses other school and profs works.Few of our assignments says 'UBC LING' assignment.
He is a nice guy, but he is not a nice prof. Difficult to understand what he said.
Not easy. He didn't overly explain anything and expected you know everything in the book as well. Homework and test were really hard.

his lecture notes are just not enough for students to understand all the materials well. homework are really really hard. his answer keys or hint are just too long to read and feeling not useful at all. He is poor in explaining students' questions. materials are too hard to understand as a 200 level course, he even cannot explain well too.

he is nice, but you have to work hard otherwise you will feel completely lost since he just put very little info on his lecture notes, but you will be tested everything in the books. Overall he is a nice guy.
